name: ComparableUtils
javadoc:
/** * Helper translating {@link Comparable#compareTo} results to booleans. * *

Example: {@code boolean x = ComparableUtils.is(myComparable).lessThanOrEqualTo(otherComparable)}

* *

#ThreadSafe#

* * @since 3.10 */

members.name: between
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Creates a predicate to test if {@code [b <= a <= c]} or {@code [b >= a >= c]} where the {@code a} is the tested object.
 *
 * @param b the object to compare to the tested object
 * @param c the object to compare to the tested object
 * @param <A> type of the test object
 * @return a predicate for true if the tested object is between b and c
 */

tags.name: b
tags.content: the object to compare to the tested object

tags.name: c
tags.content: the object to compare to the tested object

tags.name: <A>
tags.content: type of the test object
predicate for true if the tested object is between b and c

/**
 * Creates a predicate to test if {@code (b < a < c)} or {@code (b > a > c)} where the {@code a} is the tested object.
 *
 * @param b the object to compare to the tested object
 * @param c the object to compare to the tested object
 * @param <A> type of the test object
 * @return a predicate for true if the tested object is between b and c and not equal to those
 */

tags.name: b
tags.content: the object to compare to the tested object

predicate for true if the tested object is between b and c and not equal to those

members.name: max
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns the greater of two {@link Comparable} values, ignoring null. *

* For three or more values, use {@link ObjectUtils#max(Comparable...)}. *

* * @param Type of what we are comparing. * @param comparable1 the first comparable, may be null. * @param comparable2 the second comparable, may be null. * @return the largest of {@code comparable1} and {@code comparable2}. * @see ObjectUtils#max(Comparable...) * @since 3.13.0 */

tags.name: <A>
tags.content: Type of what we are comparing.

tags.name: comparable1
tags.content: the first comparable, may be null.

tags.name: comparable2
tags.content: the second comparable, may be null.
largest of {@code comparable1} and {@code comparable2}.

members.code:
/** * Returns the greater of two {@link Comparable} values, ignoring null. *

* For three or more values, use {@link ObjectUtils#max(Comparable...)}. *

* * @param Type of what we are comparing. * @param comparable1 the first comparable, may be null. * @param comparable2 the second comparable, may be null. * @return the largest of {@code comparable1} and {@code comparable2}. * @see ObjectUtils#max(Comparable...) * @since 3.13.0 */ public static > A max(final A comparable1, final A comparable2) { return ObjectUtils.compare(comparable1, comparable2, false) > 0 ? comparable1 : comparable2; }

members.name: min
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns the lesser of two {@link Comparable} values, ignoring null. *

* For three or more values, use {@link ObjectUtils#min(Comparable...)}. *

* * @param Type of what we are comparing. * @param comparable1 the first comparable, may be null. * @param comparable2 the second comparable, may be null. * @return the smallest of {@code comparable1} and {@code comparable2}. * @see ObjectUtils#min(Comparable...) * @since 3.13.0 */

tags.name: <A>
tags.content: Type of what we are comparing.

tags.name: comparable1
tags.content: the first comparable, may be null.

tags.name: comparable2
tags.content: the second comparable, may be null.
smallest of {@code comparable1} and {@code comparable2}.

members.code:
/** * Returns the lesser of two {@link Comparable} values, ignoring null. *

* For three or more values, use {@link ObjectUtils#min(Comparable...)}. *

* * @param Type of what we are comparing. * @param comparable1 the first comparable, may be null. * @param comparable2 the second comparable, may be null. * @return the smallest of {@code comparable1} and {@code comparable2}. * @see ObjectUtils#min(Comparable...) * @since 3.13.0 */ public static > A min(final A comparable1, final A comparable2) { return ObjectUtils.compare(comparable1, comparable2, true) < 0 ? comparable1 : comparable2; }

name: ComparableCheckBuilder
javadoc:
/**
 * Compares objects of a given generic type {@code A}.
 *
 * @param <A> the type of objects that this object may be compared against.
 */

 * @return true if the base object is between b and c
